title: Korea’s AI chip market is now the opening bell for global stocks
excerpt: Fund managers in London, New York, and Tokyo have added a new step to their morning routine: checking South Korean stocks. Korea’s $4 trillion equity market is now offering an early read on global AI risk appetite, as swings in SK Hynix and Samsung ripple through chip stocks worldwide. “We are all Korean investors now,” […]
content_html:
TL;DR

Korea’s market now sets global AI sentiment. The Kospi-Nasdaq correlation has nearly tripled. SK Hynix’s US listing extended Korea’s influence into 24-hour trading.

Fund managers in London, New York, and Tokyo have added a new step to their morning routine: checking South Korean stocks. Korea’s $4 trillion equity market is now offering an early read on global AI risk appetite, as swings in SK Hynix and Samsung ripple through chip stocks worldwide. “We are all Korean investors now,” said Hani Redha, a London-based portfolio manager at PineBridge Investments. JPMorgan Asset Management’s chief Asia market strategist presented on Korea to the firm’s global team for the first time in his 14 years on the job.

The data confirms it. The 60-day correlation between the Kospi and the Nasdaq 100 has climbed to 0.46, near the highest in two years and almost triple its five-year average of 0.16. The linkage is even stronger during selloffs: the Nasdaq 100’s sensitivity to the Kospi during periods of Korean market weakness hit the highest level since 1990 on July 7. SK Hynix joined the trillion-dollar club earlier this year, and its US-listed shares now extend Korea’s influence into Wall Street trading hours. Redha tracks Seoul, then SK Hynix’s ADRs, then Korea-focused ETFs in New York. “It’s like almost 24-hour tracking,” he said.

The influence comes with a cost. The Kospi has become one of the world’s most volatile major benchmarks, with leveraged single-stock products amplifying swings. A Monday selloff driven by scepticism about AI demand triggered a near 9% Kospi drop that spilled into Wall Street, dragging SK Hynix’s US shares down 9.3%. The Kospi has tumbled 25% since its June peak, a $1 trillion wipeout. South Korea temporarily halted new listings of single-stock leveraged ETFs to curb the speculation.

The 💜 of EU tech

The latest rumblings from the EU tech scene, a story from our wise ol' founder Boris, and some questionable AI art. It's free, every week, in your inbox. Sign up now!

The benchmark is still up 62% for the year. South Korea committed $880 billion over a decade to chips, AI data centres, and robots, and Samsung and SK Hynix control the majority of global memory chip supply. As long as that supply bottleneck holds, Korea’s sentiment-driven trading will keep setting the tone for every AI stock that depends on it. “This is the new normal investors have to accept, as long as the AI rally continues,” said Chisa Kobayashi at UBS. The question is whether a market driven by retail leverage in Seoul should be the one telling London what to think about AI.

content_html_zh:
TL;DR

韩国市场现在决定了全球人工智能情绪。 Kospi 指数与纳斯达克指数的相关性几乎增加了两倍。 SK海力士在美国上市将韩国的影响力扩展到24小时交易。

伦敦、纽约和东京的基金经理在早上的例行公事中增加了一个新步骤：检查韩国股票。 随着 SK 海力士和三星的波动波及全球芯片股，韩国 4 万亿美元的股市现在为全球人工智能风险偏好提供了初步解读。 “我们现在都是韩国投资者，”PineBridge Investments 驻伦敦的投资组合经理哈尼·雷德哈 (Hani Redha) 表示。 摩根大通资产管理公司首席亚洲市场策略师在其任职 14 年来首次向该公司的全球团队介绍了韩国的情况。

数据证实了这一点。 Kospi 指数与纳斯达克 100 指数之间的 60 天相关性已攀升至 0.46，接近两年来的最高水平，几乎是五年平均值 0.16 的三倍。 这种联系在抛售期间甚至更为强烈：在韩国市场疲软期间，纳斯达克 100 指数对 Kospi 指数的敏感度于 7 月 7 日达到 1990 年以来的最高水平。 SK海力士今年早些时候加入了万亿美元俱乐部，其在美国上市的股票现在将韩国的影响力延伸到了华尔街交易时间。 Redha 追踪首尔，然后追踪 SK 海力士的 ADR，然后追踪纽约的以韩国为重点的 ETF。 “这就像几乎 24 小时的追踪，”他说。

影响力是有代价的。 韩国综合指数已成为全球波动性最大的主要基准之一，杠杆单一股票产品放大了波动。 周一，由于对人工智能需求的怀疑而引发的抛售引发了 Kospi 下跌近 9%，并波及华尔街，拖累 SK 海力士的美国股价下跌 9.3%。 Kospi 指数自 6 月份的峰值以来已下跌 25%，损失了 1 万亿美元。 韩国暂时停止了新的单一股票杠杆ETF的上市，以遏制投机行为。

欧盟科技的💜

来自欧盟科技界的最新传言、我们明智的创始人鲍里斯的故事，以及一些值得怀疑的人工智能艺术。 每周都会免费发送到您的收件箱中。 立即注册！

该基准今年仍上涨 62%。 韩国在十年间向芯片、人工智能数据中心和机器人投入了 8800 亿美元，三星和 SK 海力士控制着全球存储芯片供应的大部分。 只要供应瓶颈持续存在，韩国的情绪驱动交易将继续为每只依赖它的人工智能股票定下基调。 瑞银 (UBS) 的小林奇萨 (Chisa Kobayashi) 表示：“只要人工智能的涨势持续下去，这就是投资者必须接受的新常态。” 问题是，首尔零售杠杆驱动的市场是否应该告诉伦敦如何看待人工智能。
